title: AI in Education

modules.title: Personalized Learning Systems
modules.sub: How AI tailors education to every individual student's needs
modules.description: Learners explore how adaptive learning platforms analyze student data to customize content, pacing, and feedback. They evaluate real-world implementations and assess the benefits and limitations of AI-driven personalization in diverse classroom contexts.

modules.title: Automated Grading and Assessment
modules.sub: Using AI to streamline feedback, evaluation, and scoring workflows
modules.description: Learners examine how automated assessment tools handle everything from multiple-choice scoring to natural language essay evaluation. They critically assess accuracy, bias, and the appropriate boundaries of automation versus human judgment in grading decisions.

modules.title: Intelligent Tutoring Systems
modules.sub: AI-powered tutors that guide learning through dynamic interaction
modules.description: Learners investigate how intelligent tutoring systems simulate one-on-one instruction by responding to student inputs in real time. They compare leading platforms, analyze their pedagogical models, and consider how educators can integrate these tools effectively alongside traditional teaching.

modules.title: Educational AI Ethics
modules.sub: Navigating fairness, privacy, and accountability in EdTech AI
modules.description: Learners confront the ethical challenges that arise when AI is deployed in educational settings, including data privacy, algorithmic bias, and equity of access. They work through frameworks for responsible AI adoption and develop criteria for evaluating whether a given tool meets ethical standards for use with students.

synopsis: Examine how AI personalizes education and supports both students and teachers. Explore adaptive learning platforms and automated assessment tools.
